title: New Gogs zero-day flaw lets hackers get remote code execution
summary: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Gogs self-hosted Git service can allow attackers to gain remote code execution (RCE) on Internet-facing instances. [...]

title: How SIEM helps MSPs reduce noise and stop threats faster
summary: MSPs don't lack security data. They struggle to separate real threats from alert noise. Kaseya explains how SIEM helps MSPs improve visibility, reduce fatigue, and respond faster. [...]

title: Romanian gets 5 years in prison for hacking Oregon govt network
summary: A Romanian national was sentenced this week to 56 months in federal prison for breaking into an Oregon state government computer network and fr cyberattacks targeting dozens of other U.S. victims. [...]

title: Webinar: Why network incidents take too long to resolve
summary: Many organizations can detect network issues quickly, but investigations and coordination often slow incident resolution. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams reduce delays and improve response times. [...]

title: Carnival Cruise confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
summary: Carnival Corporation, the world's largest cruise line operator, has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly 6 million people claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion gang in April 2026. [...]

title: Sextortionist sentenced to 33 years for targeting 145 children
summary: A Canadian man was sentenced to 33 years in prison after pleading guilty to targeting more than 145 children across the United States, some as young as 6 years old, in an eight-year-long sextortion scheme. [...]

title: GPU mining malware spreads via SEO poisoning, AI chatbots
summary: Threat actors are targeting systems with high-performance computers in an ongoing cryptojacking campaign spread through a coordinated SEO poisoning operation that also manipulated AI chatbot recommendations. [...]

title: Can you enforce strong Active Directory password rules without frustrating users?
summary: Strong Active Directory passwords don't have to come at the expense of usability. Specops Software explains how passphrases, breached password protection, and self-service resets can improve security without frustrating users. [...]

title: Glassworm botnet disrupted after resilient C2 infrastructure takedown
summary: The Glassworm botnet targeting developers in software supply-chain attacks has been disrupted after researchers took down its resilient command-and-control infrastructure relying on Solana blockchain transactions and the BitTorrent DHT network. [...]

title: FBI warns of in-person data theft attacks from extortion gang
summary: The FBI warned on Tuesday that the Silent Ransom Group (SRG) extortion gang is now targeting U.S.-based law firms in in-person data theft attacks. [...]

title: CISA gives feds 4 days to patch actively exploited cPanel plugin flaw

title: Dutch police arrests suspect linked to Ajax football club hack
summary: The Dutch National Police arrested a 35-year-old man suspected of hacking the professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) earlier this year. [...]

title: Windows 11 KB5089573 update released with performance improvements
summary: Microsoft has released the KB5089573 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 versions 25H2 and 24H2, which comes with 30 changes, including performance and reliability improvements. [...]

title: KnowledgeDeliver flaw exploited as a zero-day to install web shells

title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
